name: Ordinary Sambar (Kuzhambu)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Red Gram Dal (Pigeon Peas, Toor Dal), rinsed and picked over
A lemon-sized piece of Tamarind Pulp
1 cup Mixed Vegetables, chopped (e.g., radish, onion, okra, eggplant, sweet pepper, potato)
1 small bunch of Coriander Leaves, chopped, for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the red gram dal well and drain. Place in a heavy saucepan with 2 cups water. Bring to a boil. Once boiling, cover the pan slightly and stir several times during the last 30 minutes of cooking. Set aside without draining.
2. Soak tamarind pulp in 1 cup hot water for 15 minutes. Strain into another container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the pulp. Discard the pulp. Set juice aside.
3. Select enough vegetables to fill about 1 cup when chopped. Peel and prepare as necessary. Chop into 1/2-inch pieces. Set aside.
4. TEMPERING: Heat 1-1.5 tablespoons oil in a heavy saucepan. Add mustard seeds, asafoetida powder, fenugreek seeds, cumin seeds, halved red chili, and curry leaves.
- When mustard seeds splutter, add slit green chillies and chopped vegetables. Sauté for a couple of minutes.
- Add tamarind juice, 1 cup water, salt to taste, ground turmeric, and sambar powder. Cover and simmer over low heat until vegetables are tender.
- Add undrained cooked dal. Simmer for 5 minutes until thoroughly blended.
- If needed, make a smooth paste of rice flour in extra water (2 tbsp). Add to the sambar and cook for 2-3 minutes.
5. Garnish with chopped coriander leaves. Serve hot with rice.
